title: 第 133 節
en: The way may be traversed which is barred by " Thus shall it be till the days of Saoshyds (Saoshya«t ; Vd. XIX, 6, note 4). ' This King, this man of power.

a river springing from the deep; but one way cannot be traversed, namely, the way of the pitiless Vayu^ 78. Pairithw6 bavaiti p3.ntau yim susis p&iti g^ustav^u, aspanghidho, vtrangh&dhd, vlrs^, anamare^dikd; hau diaf a^v6 apairithw6, y6 vayaor anamare-sfdikahS : —

The way may be traversed which is barred by a serpent as big as an ox, horse-devouring, mandevouring, man-killing, and pitiless ; but one way cannot be traversed, namely, the way of the pitiless Vayu.
79. Pairithwd bavaiti pzntau yim areshd pditi akhsha^nd anamare^dikd ; haw did adv6 apairithw6, y6 vayao^ anamarejsfdikahfi : —
The way may be traversed which is barred by a brown bear, [with a white forehead, man-killing, and] pitiless; but one way cannot be traversed, namely, the way of the pitiless Vayu.

'80. Pairithw6 bavaiti psLUtau yim mashy6 gadhd paiti aevd/and anamare^dikd ; hau di// a6v6 apairithwo, y6 vayaoJ anamare^dikah^ : — The way may be traversed which is defended by a highwayman who kills at one stroke, (who stops the way and lets no one pass alive) ; but one way cannot be traversed, namely, the way of the pitiless Vayu.
81. Pairithwd bavaiti psLniau yd ha^nayau iakhravaity^u vy&zday^u ; hau di// a6vd apairithwd, yd vayao^ anamare-sfdikahfi : —

The way may be traversed which is held by a horde armed with discs, and uplifted spears (that is, carrying spears to pierce men); but one way viiL aogemaid£, 383 cannot be traversed, namely, the way of the pitiless Vayu.
8 1 bis. Azd mrao^ Ahurd Mazdau : du^khratflm apairi gaStham athr&vaya^ githSm \ 82. Yatha drv^u gaom isti, uta drvau aspem isti, uta drvau mafishinem yavanghem isti : —
The wicked acquire cattle, the wicked acquire horses, the wicked acquire sheep and corn ; but the wicked tyrant does not acquire a store of good deeds.

83. Seek ye for a store of good deeds, O Zarathu^tra, men and women ! for a store of good deeds is full of salvation, O Zarathuxtra 1 84. PSsnux gavd, pasnuj aspa, pSsnu^ erezatem zaranim, pSsnuj nar6 iiryd takhm6 : —

(For) the ox turns to dust, the horse turns to dust, silver and gold turn to dust, the valiant strong man turns to dust ; [the bodies of all men mingle with the dust. What do not mingle with the dust are the Ashem-vohft which a man recites in this world and his almsgiving to the holy and righteous] \ 85. For if there were or could be any escape from death, the first of the world, Gay6mard, king of the Mountain *, [would have escaped], scription, with a comipt paraphrase as follows : — ' Hormazd said, " The man without intelligence (that is, with a bad intelligence) . . . who has not sung the G&thaa (that is, who has not performed the sacrifice ; cf. Nirang. § 41) has no good renown on this earth nor bliss in heaven (cf. §§ 21, 22) . • ." '
» Cf. Ar<a Virif CI, 20.

' Gar-shdh, king of Mount Dam&vand (Albirfin!, Chronology, p. 28), or G^ibil, the mountainous part of Media. Later chronicles corrupted Gar-sh^h into Gil-sh&h, king of clay, which was interpreted as king of the earth.
86. Who for three thousand years kept the world free from death and old age, from hunger, thirst, and evil ^ ;
87. Yet, when death came over him, he delivered up his body and could not struggle with death.
88. Or there was H6shang, the P6shd4dian, 89. Who destroyed two-thirds of all the evil creatures of Ahriman * ;
90. Yet, when death came over him, he delivered up his body and could not struggle with death.
91. Or there was Tahmftraf, the well-armed, the son of Vlvanghat, 92. Who made the Demon of demons, Gan& Mainyd, his steed*, and extorted from him the seven kinds of writing ♦ ;
93. Yet, when death came over him, he delivered up his body and could not struggle with death.
94. Or there was G^im, the Sh6d, the good shepherd, the son of Vlvanghat ; (he was Sh6d, that is to say, shining ; he was a good shepherd, that is to say, ' Bundahw XXXIV, i, 2. • See Yt. V, 22, 23.

' See Yt. XV, 11-13. In the Sanskrit translation this is inter* preted as an allegory : ' Tahm(iraf rode on Ahriman ; that means that he subdued the bad Ahriman in himself.' Cf. Mirkhond, in the History of the Early Kings of Persia, tr. by Shea, p, 98.

^ Accordihg to Firdausi, Tahmuras obliged the D6vs he had conquered to teach him some thirty kinds of writing, the Rftmt, the T&zt, the P&rst, the Sogdht, the Chinese, the Pahlavi, Ac. According to the Mfndkhard (XXVII, 23) he brought to light the seven kinds of writing that the demon kept hidden. Hence i8 derived the legend in Albtriint, p. 28, that when Tahmuras was warned about the Deluge, ' he ordered all scientific books to be preserved for posterity, and to be buried in the least exposed place ; ' in favour of which report, Albtriln} mentions the discovery of many loads of unintelligible bark-manuscripts in buildings under ground, at Ispahan, in his own time.
VIII. ACXJEMAIDfe. 385 he kept in good condition troops of men and herds of animals) ^ ;
95. Who, for 616 years, 6 months and 13 days*, kept this world free from death and old age, and kept away greed and need from the creation of Hdrmazd ;
96. Yet, when death came over him, he delivered up his body and could not struggle with death.

97. Or there was Dah&k ', he of the evil religion, who kept the world under his tyranny during a Uiousand years, less one day, 98. And introduced into the world many ways of witchcraft and evil-doing ;
99. Yet, when death came over him, he delivered up his body and could not struggle with death.
100. Or there was FrfedAn, the Athwyan, ID I. Who smote and bound A^ Dahik, that great evil-doer * ; he put in chains the D6vs of Mizandar&n^ and introduced into the world a niunber of talismans ^ ;
102. Yet, when death came over him, he delivered up his body and could not struggle with death.
zh: 這條路途可以被通過，雖被「直到救世主時代（Saoshya«t；見《萬物知識經》第十九卷第六節註四）仍將如此」所阻擋。'這位國王，這位擁有力量的人。
一條源自深處的河流；但有一條路無法通行，即無情的婆由神（Vayu）^78 的道路。「凡具有虔誠之心者，必獲善果，彼為聰慧者、勤奮者、賢德者、謙遜者、不傲慢者；然則不具虔誠之心者，即風神（Vayu）之不敬者——」
大如牛、吞食馬匹、吞食人類、殺害人類且無情的蛇所阻擋的道路，可以被穿越；但有一條道路無法被穿越，即無情的毘由（Vayu）之道。
79. 帕亞瑞特瓦(Pairithwd)當以善思之心而來，彼若遵循阿胡拉(Ahura)之法則，則蒙福庇護而不遭毀損；然而背離帕亞瑞特瓦者，即彼違悖瓦亞烏(Vayao)之教誨之人，將遭致毀滅——
可以通行被棕熊[額頭有白斑、殺人的、無情的]所阻斷的道路；但有一條道路無法通行，即那無情的風神(Vayu)的道路。
被高速公路強盜所防守之道路得以通行，此強盜一擊而殺人（阻擋道路，不許任何人活著通過）；然而有一道路不能通行，即無慈悲之瓦尤（Vayu）之道路。
81. 對於那些遵循正道者，天智之光得以照耀；但對於那些背離正道者，則陷入黑暗之中。
持著圓盤與高舉長矛（即持矛以刺人）的武裝軍隊所把守的道路，是可以通行的；然而有一條道路卻無法通行，即無情的毘由（Vayu）之道。
81之二。阿胡拉‧瑪茲達（Ahura Mazda）言：「惟願汝等以聖火之炎焰護衛此聖歌。」 82。如同牲畜之存在者，馬匹之存在者，以及人類婦女之存在者一樣——
邪惡者獲得牲畜，邪惡者獲得馬匹，邪惡者獲得羊群與穀物；然而邪惡的暴君卻未能獲得善業的儲積。
83. 祆教徒瑣羅亞斯德啊，男人與女人們，應當為自己尋求善行的寶藏！祆教徒瑣羅亞斯德啊，善行的寶藏充滿救贖。 84. Pasnux gavd, pasnuj aspa, pāsnu^ erezatem zaranim, pāsnuj narō ūryd takhm ：——

牛化為塵埃，馬化為塵埃，銀和金化為塵埃，英勇強壯的人化為塵埃；[所有人的肉身與塵埃混合。不與塵埃混合的是人在此世所誦之《阿舍姆─沃赫圖》(Ashem-vohft)及其對聖賢之施捨]\85. 蓋若有或可能逃脫死亡，則世界之首者蓋亞馬德(Gay6mard)，山之王*[當已逃脫]，經文附以腐敗的意譯如下：——「霍馬茲德說，『無智慧之人（即具有惡劣智慧者）……未曾誦唱《蓋薩經》(G&thaa)（即未曾進行祭祀；參考《尼朗經》§41）者，在此世上無善名，在天堂亦無福樂（參考§§21、22）。』」
> 參見《阿拉伯書》第一卷第二十章
蓋爾-沙赫（Gar-shdh），丹姆萬德山的君王（見阿爾伯魯尼《年代學》第28頁），或作基比爾（Gibil），乃美地亞的山地部分。後代編年史將蓋爾-沙赫訛傳為吉爾-沙赫（Gil-sh&h），取義為「泥土之王」，遂被解釋為大地之王。
86. 誰曾經三千年來使世界遠離死亡與衰老、飢餓、口渴與邪惡^；
87. 然而，當死亡臨於他時，他交付了他的身軀，無法與死亡相爭。
88. 或者有赫尚（Hôshang），波什陀底安（Pôshdâdian），89. 他摧毀了阿里曼（Ahriman）*所有邪惡生物的三分之二；
90. 然而，當死亡降臨於他時，他交出了自己的身體，無法與死亡相抗爭。
91. 或者說有塔赫姆特拉夫（Tahmftraf），那位武裝精良者，弗拉凡格哈特（Vlvanghat）之子，92. 他制伏了眾魔之魔、甘迪克‧邁因尤德（Gan& Mainyd），使其成為他的駿馬*，並從他那裡強取了七種文字♦；
93. 然而，當死亡降臨於他身上時，他交出了自己的身體，卻無法與死亡抗爭。
94. 或者有吉姆（Gîm），舍德（Shêd），良善的牧人，弗拉凡格哈特之子；（他是舍德，亦即閃耀者；他是良善的牧人，亦即 《本達希什》第三十四卷，第一、二節。 ‧ 參見《雅施特》第五篇，第二十二、二十三節。
參見《雅什特經》第十五卷第11-13節。在梵文譯本中，此段被詮釋為寓言：「塔赫米拉夫騎乘在阿赫里曼身上；此乃表示他征服了自身內的邪惡阿赫里曼。」參照米爾罕德《波斯早期諸王史》，雪亞英譯本，第98頁。

^ 根據菲爾道西（Firdausi）的記載，塔赫穆拉斯強迫他所征服的諸魔（Dêvs）教授他約三十種文字，包括魯密文（Rûmî）、塔茲文（Tâzt）、波斯文（Pârst）、粟特文（Sogdht）、中文、帕拉維文（Pahlavi）等。根據《心經》（Mîndkhard）第二十七章第二十三節的記載，他揭露了惡魔所隱匿的七種文字。由此衍生出阿爾比魯尼（Albirûnî）第二十八頁所述的傳說：當塔赫穆拉斯受警告關於大洪水時，「他命令將所有科學書籍保存給後世，並埋藏在最不易暴露的地方。」為了支持這個說法，阿爾比魯尼提及在他所處的時代，於伊斯帕罕（Ispahan）的地下建築物中發現了許多堆載的難以辨認的樹皮手稿。
八、阿克謝邁代（Acxjemaide）385 他保持著良好狀態的士兵部隊和畜牲群^
95. 誰在六百一十六年、六個月又十三天*之間，使此世界免於死亡與衰老，並將貪欲與匱乏遠離於阿胡拉‧馬茲達（Ahura Mazda）的創造物；
96. 然而，當死亡降臨於他時，他交出了他的身體，無法與死亡相爭。
97. 或者有達赫克（Dahk'），那個信奉邪惡宗教的人，他以暴虐統治世界達一千年零一日，98. 並在世間引入許多巫術和邪惡行為的方式；
99. 然而，當死亡降臨於他之時，他交付了其身體，不能與死亡相爭。
100. 或者說有弗瑞德安（Frfed An），那位阿特維亞人（Athwyan），我說的是他。他擊敗並束縛了阿‧達希克（A^ Dahik），那個偉大的作惡者；他用鎖鏈囚禁了米贊達蘭（Mizandar&n）的諸魔（D6vs），並將許多護符（talismans）引入了世間。
102. 然而當死亡降臨於他時，他交出了自己的身體，無法與死亡相抗爭。
